items.title: Alleged Scattered Spider hacker arrested in Finland
items.description: The 19-year-old dual American-Estonian national has been extradited to the US, where he is facing federal charges.

items.title: Security guard rescued from building basement eight days after Venezuela earthquakes
items.description: An international rescue team saved a 43-year-old man from a collapsed shopping centre in Venezuela's La Guaira on Thursday, eight days after back-to-back earthquakes devastated the coastal region. Rescuers first made contact with the survivor, Hernán Alberto Gil Flores, at the weekend and were able to pass him water and liquid nutrients through a narrow shaft as they navigated the complex rescue operation.

items.title: Lions hold on for win over Geelong in Kardinia classic
items.description: Brisbane Lions earn a second straight win over Geelong at Kardinia Park in a high-scoring epic, as the Lions jump into the top four.

items.title: South Korea's currency chief says in talks with Japan, allies
Mexico: The 2026 World Cup's best host?
items.description: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Many visitors rave about the atmosphere and wish there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to the United States.

items.title: Gazans take stock of the war 1,000 days after its start
items.description: 1,000 days since the massacres of October 7th means 1,000 since total war was unleashed on the more than 2 million residents of the Gaza strip. Israeli retaliation to the Hamas massacre is said to have killed more than 73,000 people and wounded many more, including since the ceasefire of October 2025. Benjamin Netanyahu hopes to expand the security zone further and to cover 70% of Gaza, despite a UN warning that this is intensifying risks to civilians and further constraining humanitarian efforts.

items.title: Firefighters battle blazes scorching southern France after record heatwave
items.description: Hundreds of firefighters battled wildfires raging in southern France on Thursday after weeks of dry weather and record temperatures, with a further heatwave forecast to hit the country in the coming days.

items.title: Bayern Munich signs Morocco star Ismael Saibari from PSV Eindhoven
items.description: Bayern Munich has signed Morocco’s World Cup standout Ismael Saibari from Dutch champion PSV Eindhoven for a reported fee of around 55 million euros

items.title: Teen accused of abducting 17yo with intent to force marriage
items.description: A teen has been accused of abducting a 17-year-old girl from near a Melbourne school with the intention of forcing her to marry him.

items.title: Privacy Commissioner ordered to hand over report on Amex security failures
items.description: The Privacy Commissioner has been ordered by parliament to hand over a secret report detailing its investigation into American Express's technology security failures, after using gag orders and legal threats to keep it confidential.

items.title: WHO says 120 dead in Sudan's latest cholera outbreak
items.description: The UN health body says another 1,102 suspected cases have been reported since May in isolated war zones across the country.

items.title: First Islamic‑theological faculty at a public university
items.description: The University of Münster is the first public university in Europe to establish a faculty of Islamic theology, which is attracting international attention.

items.title: Turkish police beat us with iron rods before we lost limbs to frostbite, Afghans say
items.description: The young migrants heading to Europe say they were stripped and had their hands tied in freezing temperatures.

items.title: Roberto Sanchez challenges Fujimori's win in Peru
items.description: Peru's electoral authority said this week that Keiko Fujimori was elected with 50.13% of the vote, compared to Roberto Sanchez's 49.86%

items.title: Almost 1.2 million of Spain's undocumented migrants apply for regularisation
items.description: Almost 1.2 million undocumented migrants have applied for legal status in Spain, with more than 600,000 requests already being processed, figures released on Thursday show. Initial forecasts predict that around half a million applications will be successful.

items.title: UN asks Pakistan to reverse ‘reconstructions’ at Taxila sites
items.description: In March, a visitor shared information and pictures with Pakistan’s Permanent Delegate to UNESCO in Paris, highlighting reconstruction works by the Punjab archaeology department

items.title: Venezuela quake survivor pulled out alive after eight days
items.description: Hernán Gil was trapped under a collapsed multi-storey car park, and encouraged them as they inched closer to him.

items.title: Salman Rushdie on survival, censorship and the power of fiction
items.description: More than three decades after the fatwa over "The Satanic Verses" and nearly four years after surviving a knife attack that left him blind in one eye, Salman Rushdie returns with "The Eleventh Hour", his first work of fiction since the assault. Speaking to arts24's Eve Jackson at Porto's new BABELL literary gathering, the celebrated novelist reflects on resilience, censorship, mortality and why storytelling remains one of humanity's greatest acts of freedom.

items.title: Trump urges acting spy chief to declassify records, including on 2020 U.S. election
items.description: Bill Pulte, a political loyalist with no national security experience, was appointed as acting director of national intelligence last month; he has reportedly already ​moved to cut hundreds of intelligence jobs

items.title: New UN report links Joseph Kabila more closely to AFC/M23
items.description: A new United Nations Group of Experts report paints the clearest picture yet of the AFC/M23’s military strength and political ambitions in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo, while highlighting the movement’s increasingly close ties with former President Joseph Kabila.

items.title: What is the Society of St Pius X? Why Pope Leo excommunicated its members
items.description: The Vatican declares 'schism' in Catholic Church after bishops were ordained without Pope Leo XIV's approval.

items.title: Portugal vs Croatia: FIFA World Cup – Ronaldo-Modric, prediction, team news
items.description: A last-16 meeting against either Spain or Austria awaits the winners of the round of 32 clash in Toronto.

items.title: Iran turns Khamenei funeral into show of power
items.description: Iran's leadership is planning a six-day funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei starting July 4, in what officials say will be the largest gathering in Tehran's history — and a demonstration of strength at home and abroad.

items.title: Paris des Arts: Special edition in Fez, Morocco
items.description: This week, Paris des Arts turns its cameras to Fez, the Moroccan imperial city, for the 29th edition of the Festival of World Sacred Music, which celebrates the dialogue between cultures and spiritualities.

items.title: Spain immigration scheme sees 1.2 million apply for legal status
items.description: Spain immigration scheme sees more than a million applications, with Latin Americans leading the numbers at 67 percent.

items.description: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

items.title: Canadian boy, 11, dies of rabies after waking to bat on his face
items.description: Rabies infections are rare in Canada. There have been 28 human deaths due to rabies in the country since 1924.

items.title: Watch: Jammu and Kashmir should become a ‘bridge of peace’ between India and Pakistan: Mehbooba Mufti
items.description: Peoples Democratic Party president Mehbooba Mufti on July 1 suggested that the Centre make Jammu and Kashmir a gateway between South and Central Asia, and use its geographical location to strengthen ties with Pakistan and China.

items.title: The US deported them to Venezuela
items.description: They landed on Flight 164 just before the twin quakes. Family members are desperately searching for them.

items.title: France's far-right party National Rally probed for embezzlement of EU funds
items.description: The European justice is keeping a close eye at France's far-right National Rally. A series of searches were carried out as part of a probe into alleged embezzlement of funds, targeting the far-right group which Jordan Bardella once sat in at the European Parliament. Meanwhile, Jordan Bardella has denounced it as judicial persecution.

items.title: Milei-linked Fundación Faro told to name donors behind US$4.8mn windfall
items.description: Fundación Faro presented itself as a platform of ideas. But the libertarian think tank’s opaque finances, links to key Milei insiders and 400-fold surge in donations have left the organisation facing uncomfortable questions from regulators and judges. Leer más

items.description: German prosecutors believe the Ukrainian national suspected of sabotaging Baltic Sea gas pipelines in 2022 acted "on the orders of state authorities in Ukraine."

U.S. and Iran hold separate meetings in Qatar and agree to continue discussions
items.description: U.S. and Iranian negotiators met separately on Wednesday with Qatari and Pakistani mediators, with "positive progress made," and they agreed to continue discussions, host Qatar said.

items.title: Coalition MP calls out gambling advertising carve-out
items.description: Liberal MP Simon Kennedy argues the federal government's proposed changes to gambling advertising laws contain a streaming loophole.

items.title: Ivory Coast fans disappointed after 2-1 Norway knockout
items.description: Fans of the Ivory Coast soccer team gathered on Tuesday in Abidjan, the capital of the Ivory Coast, to watch their team play against Haaland’s Norway. But a slight tap from Haaland’s left foot in the 86th minute was the difference as Norway beat Ivory Coast 2-1 on Tuesday.

items.title: Iran will not cooperate with other countries to de-mine Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The Iranian Foreign Ministry said in a statement that, according to the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding (MoU), the mine clearance work in the Strait of Hormuz will be carried out by Iran alone, and Iran will not cooperate with any other country on this matter.

items.title: ‘Excited’ Donald Trump takes first flight on Qatar-gifted Air Force One
items.description: "Frankly, we couldn't build a plane like this because we wouldn't be willing to spend the kind of money necessary. They spent top dollars," U.S. President Donald Trump said

items.title: El Nino effect: Challenges to adaptation and resilience
items.description: Europe and countries in South Asia, including India, are reeling under extreme heat and monsoon deficit; heat stress has thrown life out of gear across the world making the vulnerable people the worst victim

items.description: Critics say it could hasten forced assimilation, lead to targeting of critics outside China. Beijing rejects charges.

items.description: Fault Lines investigates allegations of systematic torture of Palestinian prisoners and detainees in Israeli custody.

items.title: From rural Tamil Nadu to Wall Street: Chef Vijay Kumar on ringing the Nasdaq closing bell
items.description: In this exclusive essay for The Hindu, Vijay Kumar, the chef behind New York’s Michelin-starred Semma, reflects on his journey from a village near Natham in Tamil Nadu to global recognition, and what ringing the Nasdaq closing bell meant to him and the future of Tamil cuisine

items.title: Ukraine's attacks forcing 'Russians to realise the war is really happening'
items.description: Ukraine has stepped up long-range drone attacks inside Russia in recent weeks, targeting energy infrastructure and military targets. Russian officials have reported repeated strikes in border regions, while Moscow has said its air defences had intercepted hundreds of drones from Ukraine in recent days. This comes as a new study shows that Russia is bearing the brunt of military losses, with a Russia/Ukraine casualty rate of 8 to 1 in 2026. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more on what to know.

items.title: Gaza war’s 1,000 days: 90% of strip ‘destroyed’, 80% ‘seized’ by Israel
items.description: In 1,000 days since October 7, 2023, Gaza lies in ruins, Board of Peace falters and Israel expands control of enclave.

items.title: KNDS: German-French tank manufacturer postpones IPO
items.description: The German government had been set to purchase a 40% stake in KNDS, but the prerequisite stock market flotation has been postponed. Meanwhile, a German drone start-up has raised $1 billion in fresh funding.

items.title: Syria explosion updates: Bomb blast kills 5 people in Damascus
items.description: ⁠An improvised explosive device detonates in a popular ​Damascus cafe as Syrian security forces hunt for attackers.

items.title: Zimbabwean citizen recounts leaving South Africa over anti-migrant sentiment
items.description: Around 25,000 migrants have left South Africa in recent weeks over rising anti-migrant sentiment that has resulted in the deaths of four people. One returnee from Zimbabwe recounts his experience.

items.title: Trump administration and Anthropic have not discussed the government taking a stake in it, source says
Algerians vote in parliamentary elections overshadowed by candidate bans
items.description: Algerians on Thursday voted in parliamentary elections amid concerns over turnout after a campaign season marked by low attendance at official campaign events. Electoral authorities barred 269 candidates from running for the 407-seat lower house of parliament ahead of the vote.

items.title: Stores assess damage after looting in South Africa's anti-foreigner protests
items.description: Ransacked shelves and discarded packaging were all that remained inside several shops near the port city of Durban Wednesday after looting during protests demanding that undocumented foreign nationals leave South Africa.

items.title: German coalition agrees on sweeping reform package
items.description: After its first year in office, Germany's coalition government is seeking to revive a sluggish economy and push through key changes with its broad package of reforms.

items.title: Wole Soyinka university theatre fosters new talent in Nigeria and beyond
items.description: At the University of Ibadan in southwestern Nigeria, the Wole Soyinka Theatre stands out as a true breeding ground for talent. For decades, it has trained artists who have shaped theatre, film, and culture in Nigeria and beyond.

items.title: Vatican excommunicates six ultraconservative bishops over ordination
items.description: The Society of Saint Pius X, which has around 600,000 followers around the world, comprises fundamentalist Catholics who strongly oppose the liberal reforms imposed by the Vatican II Council in the 1960s

items.title: US rail boom still lags behind cars and planes

items.title: Sudan's RSF committed ethnic cleansing, Amnesty report alleges
items.description: Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Forces committed crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing during their campaign to seize El-Fasher between 2024 and 2025, according to a new report from Amnesty International released on Wednesday.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Belgium and England escape elimination against Senegal and DR Congo
items.description: We've now seen two of the most thrilling games at the World Cup so far, with tears of joy for some, and of sadness and shock for others. Senegal were four minutes away from securing a place in the Round of 16 before Belgium turned their 2-0 deficit around to equalise, before winning in extra time. DR Congo stunned England when they opened the score in the seventh minute and were a threat throughout the tie. But it was Harry Kane who saved the day for the Three Lions as he scored both goals to seal a 2-1 comeback.

items.title: Zelenskyy condemns ’horrific attacks’ as Russian strikes kill 8, wound 35 in Ukraine
items.description: A Russian missile targeting infrastructure struck the central city of Dnipro, killing five people and wounding 29, Mr. Zelenskyy said on social media

items.description: Barefoot, arms raised and teeming with emotion, Ceuzany belts out the signature track from her latest album, a song dedicated to the fight against domestic violence, in a powerful voice that sends shivers through the Cape Verde bar

items.description: A preliminary report by the UN’s Independent International Scientific Panel on Artificial Intelligence said policymakers face a growing dilemma: they need robust evidence to regulate AI effectively, yet such evidence is ‌struggling to keep pace with the technology’s rapid evolution

items.description: Some residents reluctantly hung their dogs to avoid being arrested or fined, locals tell the BBC.

items.description: Dates have officially been set for France's 2027 presidential election. With less than a year to go, the country is already gearing up for Emmanuel Macron's successor. FRANCE 24 English and French teamed up to meet voters in the run-up to the crucial poll.

items.description: A fire at a hospital in the northern German town of Ludwigslust has killed two people. Many patients and staff were evacuated from the facility.

items.description: Once described as "the godfather" of French migrant camps, he has been working in a Leicestershire village.

items.title: Pape Gueye refuses Senegal return until technical staff changes
items.description: Senegal midfielder Pape Gueye has announced an indefinite break from international football following the Teranga Lions' dramatic FIFA World Cup exit to Belgium, saying he will not return to the national team while the current technical staff remain in charge.

items.title: Funerals held for 14 Pakistani children killed in tutoring center collapse
items.description: Police are investigating whether negligence during construction work caused the collapse in the eastern city of Lahore on Tuesday.

items.title: Chanel vs Dior: Why luxury’s two biggest reboots are under scrutiny
items.description: Matthieu Blazy has sparked immediate demand at Chanel, while Jonathan Anderson is taking a longer view at Dior. In a tougher luxury market, both houses are fighting harder for growth.

items.description: Widespread flooding has killed at least 59 people in Ivory Coast since the "particularly heavy" rainy season began in mid-May, a government spokesperson said Wednesday

items.title: 'Beautiful and terrible'
items.description: From Brazil to India to Egypt, the BBC asked people across the world what they think of America today.
items.source: the world's thoughts on the US at 250

items.title: Gaza patients face agonising delays for medical evacuation
items.description: An estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire began, according to Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry.

items.title: Catholic Schools NSW chief to stand aside amid corruption probe
items.description: The head of the body governing the state's catholic schools will stand aside after he was named in a corruption probe investigating donations to the NSW Liberal Party.

items.title: A week after the earthquakes, Venezuela still searches for its thousands missing
items.description: Rescue teams in Venezuela are working frantically to free a man that's been trapped in the rubble of a collapsed building for eight days. For the past seventy-two hours - emergency workers from seven countries have worked nonstop trying to free 43-year-old Hernan Gil. He's a security guard buried inside his booth in the coastal city of Catia La Mer. Rescuers say it's a miracle he's still alive. Meanwhile the Venezuelan government has updated the death toll from that deadly quake - at least 2,295 people have been killed and more than 11,000 injured. The United Nations says its procuring 10,000 body bags for the country.

items.title: Xenophobic attacks: Is South Africa still safe for Africans? (Africanews Debates)
items.description: Thousands of people have taken part in demonstrations across South Africa calling for tougher action against undocumented migrants, in what some accounts describe as the largest anti-immigration mobilisation in recent years.

items.title: Nine Thai monks killed after 11-year-old driver collides with procession
items.description: The group of 35 monks and five lay followers were walking by the roadside when the truck ploughed into them.

items.title: Danantara Indonesia says financial report is still being processed
Solomon expects 'tap on shoulder' in Bombers coach race
items.description: James Hird's open ambitions have done little to sway Dean Solomon, with the interim Essendon coach yet to declare his future plans.

items.title: OpenAI proposes handing Trump administration 5% stake, FT reports
Andy Burnham outlines his vision for UK economy with promise to raise living standards
items.description: Andy Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester, is likely to be the next British prime minister following Keir Starmer's resignation. Central to Burnham's platform is improving living standards that have stagnated or even fallen since the 2008 global financial crisis. But with weak public finances, how realistic is his plan? FRANCE 24's Charles Pellegrin talks to Renaud Foucart, senior lecturer in economics at Lancaster University.

items.title: Algeria votes for new parliament with turnout and cost-of-living in focus
items.description: Algerians headed to the polls on July 2 to elect a new parliament in an election shadowed by expectations of low turnout among the 25-million-person electorate. The vote is taking place amid controversy of the government disqualifying roughly a third of potential candidates, with some saying they were blocked from competing for major constituencies. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin tells us more about how the elections works, and in what context they're taking place.

items.title: Ebola outbreak: UN projects $3.6 billion cost for Africa in worst case
items.description: In a report released Tuesday, the United Nations Development Programme outlined several scenarios for the economic cost of the ongoing Ebola virus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. In the worst case, the epidemic could lead to a $3.6 billion cost for the African continent.

items.title: Mercosur summit exposes divisions over implementation of EU trade agreement
items.description: Mercosur bloc expresses solidarity with Venezuela after devastating twin quakes; Group's disagreements over EU trade deal aired. Leer más

items.title: Camping in France: Is the spirit gone?
items.description: As summer vacation season gets underway here in France, we take a closer look at camping. France holds the record for the most campsites in Europe. We look at camping's aristocratic origins, its popularity and how it is currently changing with an ongoing battle between tents, bungalows and camping cars.

items.title: Israel and Gaza mark 1,000 days since the October 7 attacks and the start of the war
items.description: Israel is marking the 1,000th day since Hamas's October 7, 2023 attack with calls for a state commission of inquiry into the unprecedented assault that triggered the war in Gaza. But Netanyau's government has long refused to establish such a commission. Meanwhile, Gazans still face attacks despite the truce, while Israeli intelligence warns that Hamas is regrouping. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky tells us about the mood in Israel, and the current situation in Gaza.

items.title: US Ambassador Peter Lamelas: ‘United States stands shoulder to shoulder with Argentina’
items.description: US Ambassador to Argentina Peter Lamelas proclaims support for President Javier Milei and explains his own "America Dream" in a speech marking 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence. Leer más

items.description: President Javier Milei rode to power vowing to destroy the political elite he derided as “the caste.” Now, after a string of corruption scandals, he’s turning to one of its most recognisable figures. Leer más

items.title: Thailand: Child crashes vehicle into group of monks
items.description: An 11-year-old boy crashed a pickup truck belonging to his parents into a group of monks on a pilgrimage, police said. Eight were killed in the incident and 14 more were hospitalized in Mukdahan province.

items.title: Bomb blast in central Damascus cafe kills several
items.description: An explosion in a cafe in central Damascus killed six people on Thursday and injured 22, Syrian authorities said. Syrian state television said the blast was caused by an explosive device.

items.description: The EU has moved to curb what it sees as unfair competition from mostly Chinese online retailers by imposing its levy. It follows a similar move from the US.

items.title: Messi returns 'home' to lead Argentina World Cup charge in Miami

items.title: Germany news: Coalition agrees package to boost growth
items.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz says his coalition has cleared a reform package as pressure grows to deliver growth. Meanwhile, a top court rejects complaints against the criminalization of childlike sex dolls. DW has more.

items.description: While the popularity of the World Cup continues to grow, organizers FIFA have arguably never been less popular. So why does football still need FIFA? And is there any way out?

items.description: The New York City mayor, dined with his wife Rama Duwaji and his parents, filmmaker Mira Nair and scholar Mahmood Mamdani, at Chennai-based Chef Regi Mathew’s restaurant, Chatti, in Manhattan, which showcases Kerala’s toddy shop cuisine

items.title: Ramaphosa heads to Kinshasa as Africa rallies behind DRC’s Ebola fight
items.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa arrived in Kinshasa on Thursday for a high-level African Union solidarity mission aimed at reinforcing continental support for the Democratic Republic of the Congo's response to its latest Ebola outbreak.

items.title: School worker sexually assaulted girls in Adelaide Hills, court told
items.description: A contract worker accused of indecently assaulted two students at an Adelaide Hills school he worked at has appeared in court for the first time.

items.title: Gold smuggling from DRC's Ituri province to Uganda on the rise, says UN
items.description: Gold smuggling from DR Congo to Uganda is on the rise, according to a new UN report. Experts assessing the situation in the DRC for sanctions review pointed to discrepancies between Uganda’s stated gold production and its export levels in 2025.

items.title: Uganda crackdown deepens as opposition leader faces treason trial
items.description: Uganda's political crackdown is intensifying, with opposition leader Kizza Besigye appearing in court on treason charges as concerns grow over a widening assault on dissent.

items.title: Unprecedented images show how Russia is using drones from the war in Ukraine in Africa
items.description: Russian mercenaries from the Africa Corps, which has replaced the well-known Wagner Group in providing support to the Malian army, are deploying new combat drones in Mali. Frequently found on the frontlines in Ukraine, these drones are now entering the Sahel region – an environment for which they were not designed.

items.description: Turkish stand-up comedian Deniz Goktas was detained on his return to Turkey, days after a video he posted online gained traction. The clip included references to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and religion.

items.description: The EU’s central bank is preparing a digital euro to cut dependence on Visa, Mastercard and Apple Pay. Success hinges on giving consumers an easy-to-use payment system without prompting a flight from bank savings.

items.description: On Tuesday former German Chancellor Merkel presented the painting for the official portrait gallery of the country's heads of government. She had previously inspired many artists.

items.description: Protesters are clearly unwelcome ahead of the upcoming NATO summit in Ankara. Amid a strict public gatherings ban, 225 human rights and environmental activists as well as journalists have already been arrested.

items.title: Vatican excommunicates ultraconservative breakaway bishops in first crisis for Pope
items.description: The Vatican excommunicated six bishops from the ultraconservative Society of St Pius X , while warning that any lay believers who "formally adhere" to the breakaway Catholic group would suffer the same fate. The Vatican decree comes a day after the traditionalist group consecrated four new bishops, openly defying a plea from Pope Leo XIV to desist and exacerbating a rift dating from 1988 when the group earlier consecrated four bishops. FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta tells us more about this story.

items.description: Ballistic and cruise missiles and drones were used in what Kyiv's mayor, Vitali Klitschko, said was the largest attack on the city since the war began.

items.title: Cattle sale in Alice Springs nets $7m as market surges
items.description: The strong results at the Alice Springs sale reflect the overall Australian cattle market, which has surged in recent weeks.

items.title: Major milk company fined for misleading consumers about fresh milk
items.description: One of Australia's largest milk providers has been fined by the consumer watchdog for misleading consumers with "fresh milk" labels on products that contained substantial amounts of powdered milk.

items.title: Russian strikes kill 21 in biggest ever attack on Kyiv, mayor says
items.description: “Kyiv is under attack from ballistic missiles and UAVs,” Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko said on Telegram, adding that blasts could be heard across the city.

items.title: Danish Refugee Council on importance of coordinated response to Ebola, wider humanitarian crisis
items.description: The Danish Refugee Council has warned that the latest Ebola outbreak in DR Congo is complicating an already strained humanitarian situation. In a recent report, the NGO stressed the response to the health emergency "must not hollow out the aid programming that is holding communities together". Speaking to FRANCE 24, Secretary-General Charlotte Slente lamented a "funding crisis" that had already begun before the Ebola outbreak, adding that with the arrival of the virus, "there's a risk these resources are now turned into a response to Ebola".

items.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
items.description: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

items.title: 'Immense breach of human rights': WA strip searches double in three years
items.description: The number of people strip searched by police officers in Western Australia more than doubles in three years, leading some to label the practice a human rights violation.

items.title: Sudanese army claims recapture of Kulbus, key Darfur town on Chadian border
items.description: Sudanese armed forces say they have retaken the town of Kulbus in western Darfur - their biggest battlefield gain in the region since the fall of El-Fasher last year. Kulbus lies on a vital corridor near the border with Chad.

items.title: Shipping companies, unions still consider Hormuz a warzone
items.description: The Strait of Hormuz was first designated a warlike operations area on March 5, after ships trying to cross the vital energy passageway were attacked

items.title: 'One-way guest': Document that raised questions over Adorni and his wife's travel
items.description: The official document that raised awkward questions for the Milei government, months before the former Cabinet chief resigned. Leer más

items.title: Controversial penalty ends Senegal’s FIFA World Cup run against Belgium
items.description: Late VAR penalty leads to fury as Senegal blows 2-0 lead, loses 3-2 to Belgium in dramatic World Cup round of 32 exit.

items.title: State cracks down on sale of potentially deadly 'nangs'
items.description: After a 7.30 investigation exposed the increasing dangers of nitrous oxide use, NSW has announced reforms will come into place shortly.

items.title: 'Grave breach of trust': Former principal sent to prison
items.description: A retired Tasmanian school principal has been sentenced to four years in prison for child sex abuse.

items.title: US job growth slows in June; hospitality sheds roles despite World Cup
items.description: Labour force participation dropped to its lowest level since March 2021, reaching 61.5 percent in June.

items.title: Lautaro Martínez or Julián Álvarez? Scaloni weighs Messi's strike partner for Cape Verde clash
items.description: Argentina's coach must decide who will lead the line alongside Lionel Messi as the world champions prepare for Friday's last-32 clash with Cape Verde. Leer más

items.title: From Truman's pension to Trump's billions
items.description: Historians say Trump's $2.2bn income last year is unprecedented and blurs the line on conflicts of interest.
items.source: a White House windfall unmatched by any president

items.title: At least six killed in explosion inside central Damascus cafe
items.description: Blast at a cafe reportedly kills five people and injures many more.

items.title: Thousands march to demand illegal migrants leave South Africa
items.description: From Johannesburg to Durban, thousands marched across South Africa demanding undocumented migrants leave the country. The nationwide protests followed weeks of demonstrations by citizen-led groups that set an unofficial June 30 deadline for migrants without legal status to depart.

items.title: Milei looks to Santilli to move beyond Adorni chapter
items.description: President hopes trusted former PRO operator can help La Libertad Avanza regain the initiative after Manuel Adorni's resignation and push the scandal off the agenda. Leer más

items.title: Vatican expels members of rebel Catholic group over unapproved ordination
items.description: Vatican stresses only pope may approve bishop consecrations to safeguard apostolic ties to Jesus's original disciples.

items.title: Why has Trump refused to renew the USMCA, and what will that mean?
items.description: Trade ambassador says US will not approve trilateral agreement with Canada and Mexico 'in its current form'.

items.title: Amnesty says RSF committed ethnic cleansing in Sudan
items.description: Sudan has been mired since April 2023 in a brutal war between the army and the RSF, which has killed tens of thousands and forced millions to flee, according to the United Nations

items.title: Norway wins its 1st World Cup knockout game beating Ivory Coast 2-1
items.description: Erling Haaland scored the deciding goal in the 86th minute and Norway won a knockout game at the World Cup for the first time, advancing to the round of 16 with a 2-1 victory over Ivory Coast on Tuesday.

items.title: Six tourists killed, three others missing after boat capsizes in Pakistan's Swat Valley
items.description: The incident took place at Saifullah Lake in the Kalam Valley of Upper Swat district, a popular tourist destination

items.title: Kicillof admits last Peronist government ‘didn’t work’
items.description: Alberto Fernández government “didn’t work” and its breakdown led to the election of Javier Milei, says 2027 presidential hopeful in video call with Peronist leaders. Leer más

items.title: 9 children dead in Egypt after motorised tricycle plunges into canal
items.description: Relatives waited outside Abu Tig hospital in Egypt's southern Assiut province on Tuesday for the bodies of nine children killed when a motorised tricycle plunged into a canal. The victims were aged between 10 and 17.

items.title: Uruguay veteran Edison Cavani departs Boca Juniors
items.description: Uruguayan striker leaves Boca without having won a title and acknowledges he had "difficult moments" that prevented him from "leaving a mark" on the club. Leer más

items.title: Trump says the U.S. and Iran ‘getting along very well’
items.description: Head of the Iranian delegation said the discussions touched on the fate of frozen Iranian assets, which Tehran has demanded be released as part of the settlement.

items.title: Schismatic Catholic society sees six bishops excommunicated by Pope
items.description: The Vatican has excommunicated six bishops from the ultraconservative Society of St Pius X, while warning that any lay believers who "formally adhere" to the breakaway Catholic group would suffer the same fate. It comes a day after the traditionalist group consecrated four new bishops, openly defying a plea from Pope Leo XIV to desist and exacerbating a rift dating from 1988.

items.title: BlackRock says past defaults, next election cap Argentina rally
items.description: Argentina’s stellar bond rally is set to hit a wall as next year’s presidential elections revive concerns, says top BlackRock portfolio manager. Leer más
